7b3bc5ce-dfe1-48fb-bd71-b4e41489d1fa.content:
Here are some interesting questions to ask: 
1. What's your favorite memory?
2. If you could live in any time period, where would it be?
3. What is a skill you wish you could master?
4. If you could have dinner with any historical figure, who would it be?
5. What is the most adventurous thing you've ever done? 

These questions can spark engaging conversations!

ac28d958-59d2-46fa-b5ea-b479739f1049.content: SSE streaming, or Server-Sent Events streaming, is a technology that allows a server to push real-time updates to a web client over an HTTP connection. It is commonly used in web applications to send updates from the server to the client without the client needing to request them repeatedly. This is particularly useful for applications that require live data updates, such as news feeds, stock price updates, or notifications. SSE is built on top of the HTTP protocol and is simpler to implement than WebSockets for one-way communication from the server to the client.

Search results for '2023 electric vehicle sales trends':
In 2023, global electric vehicle sales surged to nearly 14 million units, marking a 35% increase. The U.S. saw a 7.6% EV market share, up from 5.9% the previous year. The International Energy Agency projects over 20 million EVs sold by 2025.

Sources: https://www.accio.com/business/electric-car-sales-trend-2023, https://theicct.org/publication/ev-ldv-us-major-markets-monitor-2023-june24/, https://ev-volumes.com/news/ev/global-ev-sales-for-2023/, https://www.coxautoinc.com/insights-hub/q4-2023-ev-sales/, https://www.eia.gov/todayinenergy/detail.php?id=61344

tool_calls.result:
Search results for 'Tavily API features and functions':
Tavily API offers search, extract, crawl, map, and research functionalities. It uses a single API for real-time web data access. Tavily supports low-latency, AI-driven web interactions.

Sources: https://docs.phidata.com/tools/tavily, https://docs.tavily.com/documentation/api-reference/introduction, https://tavily.com/, https://www.tavily.com/blog/tavily-101-ai-powered-search-for-developers, https://github.com/tavily-ai

cb6e44f2-60a4-4151-9d5a-78c8f7b3e48f.content: SSE streaming, or Server-Sent Events streaming, is a technology that allows a server to push real-time updates to a web client over a single HTTP connection. It is commonly used for applications that require live updates, such as news feeds, stock price updates, or notifications. Unlike WebSockets, which allow for two-way communication, SSE is a one-way communication channel where the server sends data to the client. This is achieved using the EventSource API in JavaScript, which listens for messages from the server and processes them as they arrive.

tool_calls.result:
Search results for 'Tavily API features and functions':
Tavily API offers search, extract, crawl, map, and research functionalities. It uses a single API for real-time web data access. Tavily supports low-latency, AI-driven web interactions.

Sources: https://docs.phidata.com/tools/tavily, https://docs.tavily.com/documentation/api-reference/introduction, https://tavily.com/, https://www.tavily.com/blog/tavily-101-ai-powered-search-for-developers, https://github.com/tavily-ai

02c0b970-4ff3-4e46-aede-4832d02a5f89.content: SSE streaming, or Server-Sent Events, is a technology that allows a server to push real-time updates to web clients over HTTP. It enables a one-way communication channel where the server can send updates to the client automatically, without the client needing to request them. This is commonly used for applications that require real-time data updates, such as live sports scores, stock price updates, or social media feeds. SSE is supported by most modern web browsers and is simpler to implement than WebSockets for many use cases.

f126e7b8-721a-4a29-9a27-5f2734ad4d7c.content: SSE streaming, or Server-Sent Events, is a technology that allows a server to push real-time updates to web clients over HTTP. It enables a one-way communication channel where the server can send updates to the client automatically, without the client needing to request them. This is commonly used for applications that require real-time data updates, such as live sports scores, stock price updates, or social media feeds. SSE is supported by most modern web browsers and is simpler to implement than WebSockets for many use cases.
